items.title: Trees & BSTs
items.description: A tree is a connected, acyclic, single-parent graph; graph algorithms (DFS, BFS) work on trees too.

items.title: Stacks & Queues
items.description: BFS uses a queue; DFS uses a stack. The same algorithm skeleton picks the worklist ADT.

items.title: Heaps & Priority Queues
items.description: Dijkstra and A* pop the lowest-cost frontier node next, which is the priority queue's job.

items.title: Dictionaries & Hash Maps
items.description: An adjacency list is typically a `dict[node, list[neighbor]]`; the `visited` set is a hash set.

items.title: Recursion
items.description: DFS, topo-sort and many graph algorithms are naturally recursive (with an iterative form for deep graphs).
